title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. dentata
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. dentata, also known as the toothed bladder fern, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cystopteris fragilis subsp. dentata include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and can be consumed as a tea or taken as a supplement to support its medicinal properties.
